evals.prompt: I've just provisioned a fresh EKS cluster on AWS and I want to install ArgoCD on it in HA mode so my platform team can use it to manage all our production workloads. We use GitHub for our git repos and we'll have around 50 apps to start with. What do I need to do to get a production-grade ArgoCD setup running?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step HA ArgoCD installation for EKS: kubectl apply of ha/install.yaml pointing to v3.3.8, initial admin password retrieval, argocd-cm with url and resource.exclusions, argocd-rbac-cm with policy.default: role:readonly, GitHub repo secret using SSH or GitHub App auth, an EKS cluster secret using IRSA config, an AppProject for platform workloads, and a root App-of-Apps Application. Should include security hardening notes (delete initial secret, TLS config, disable admin after SSO). Should mention ARGOCD_CONTROLLER_REPLICAS for app controller sharding.

assertions.name: uses-ha-manifest
assertions.description: Uses the HA install manifest path (ha/install.yaml), not the standard install.yaml

assertions.name: correct-version
assertions.description: References ArgoCD v3.3.8 specifically in the install command or manifest URL

assertions.name: rbac-default-readonly
assertions.description: argocd-rbac-cm sets policy.default to role:readonly

assertions.name: eks-irsa-cluster-secret
assertions.description: EKS cluster secret uses awsAuthConfig with clusterName and roleARN (IRSA pattern)

assertions.name: repo-secret-label
assertions.description: Repository secret includes the label argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repository

assertions.name: delete-initial-admin-secret
assertions.description: Explicitly mentions deleting or rotating argocd-initial-admin-secret as a security step

assertions.name: named-appproject
assertions.description: Creates a named AppProject rather than relying on the default project

assertions.name: app-of-apps-pattern
assertions.description: Includes or recommends an App-of-Apps root Application for GitOps bootstrap

evals.prompt: I need to set up OIDC SSO for our ArgoCD instance using Okta. Our platform team should be admins, our dev teams should only be able to sync apps in their own project (not delete or create anything), and everyone else who logs in should be read-only. We have Okta groups: okta-platform-admins, okta-dev-team-frontend, okta-dev-team-backend.
evals.expected_output: Complete argocd-cm with oidc.config block for Okta (issuer URL, clientID/Secret references, requestedScopes including groups, requestedIDTokenClaims for groups). Complete argocd-rbac-cm with: policy.default: role:readonly, platform-admin role with full access, developer role limited to sync/get in their project, group bindings mapping okta groups to roles. Should reference storing clientSecret in argocd-secret rather than plaintext. Should note to disable admin user after SSO is confirmed working.

assertions.name: uses-oidc-config-not-dex
assertions.description: Uses oidc.config in argocd-cm (not dex.config) — Okta supports direct OIDC without Dex

assertions.name: secret-reference-not-plaintext
assertions.description: ClientSecret referenced via $oidc.okta.clientSecret or similar secret reference (not hardcoded plaintext)

assertions.name: policy-default-readonly
assertions.description: argocd-rbac-cm sets policy.default: role:readonly

assertions.name: all-three-groups-mapped
assertions.description: All three Okta groups (okta-platform-admins, okta-dev-team-frontend, okta-dev-team-backend) are bound to roles

assertions.name: developer-role-sync-get-only
assertions.description: Developer role is limited to sync and get actions — no create, update, or delete

assertions.name: groups-scope-requested
assertions.description: requestedScopes includes 'groups' so ArgoCD receives group membership claims from Okta

assertions.name: disable-admin-after-sso
assertions.description: Mentions disabling the admin user (admin.enabled: false) after SSO is confirmed working

evals.prompt: I want to create an AppProject called 'payments' for our payments team. They should only be able to deploy to the payments-prod and payments-staging namespaces on our main cluster. Their git repos all live under https://github.com/acme-corp/payments-*. They need to be able to create Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, HorizontalPodAutoscalers, and Namespaces but nothing else. The payments team lead should have admin on this project, and developers should only be able to sync.
evals.expected_output: Complete AppProject YAML named 'payments' with: sourceRepos restricted to https://github.com/acme-corp/payments-* (wildcard), destinations limited to payments-prod and payments-staging namespaces on kubernetes.default.svc, clusterResourceWhitelist with only Namespace kind, namespaceResourceWhitelist allowing only Deployment/Service/ConfigMap/HPA, project roles with payments-admin (full) and payments-developer (sync/get only), finalizer on the AppProject. Should use resourceWhitelist pattern correctly.

assertions.name: sourcerepos-wildcard
assertions.description: sourceRepos uses the wildcard https://github.com/acme-corp/payments-* (not a specific repo or '*')

assertions.name: destinations-both-namespaces
assertions.description: Destinations include both payments-prod AND payments-staging namespaces

assertions.name: cluster-resource-namespace-only
assertions.description: clusterResourceWhitelist allows only Namespace kind (not ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, or '*')

assertions.name: namespace-resource-four-kinds
assertions.description: Namespace-scoped resource allowlist covers all four: Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, HorizontalPodAutoscaler

assertions.name: two-project-roles
assertions.description: Defines two project roles: one admin/lead role and one developer role

assertions.name: developer-role-sync-only
assertions.description: Developer role policies are limited to sync and/or get — no create or delete

assertions.name: finalizer-present
assertions.description: AppProject has the finalizer resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io to prevent accidental deletion

assertions.name: destination-in-cluster
assertions.description: Destination server is https://kubernetes.default.svc (in-cluster reference)
